task: There is a bug in src/utils.js: parseDate() returns NaN for valid ISO date strings like '2026-06-14'. Find the cause and fix it.

input.query: parseDate returns NaN for ISO date strings in src/utils.js

input.command: node -e "import('./src/utils.js').then(m => { const t = m.parseDate('2026-06-14'); console.log('result:', t, 'isNaN:', isNaN(t), 'date:', new Date(t).toISOString()); })"
input.description: Verify parseDate fix with ISO date string

input.learning: parseDate() in src/utils.js parses ISO-8601 date strings (YYYY-MM-DD) by splitting on '-'. It does not accept slash-delimited formats like 'YYYY/MM/DD' — callers must pre-normalize. The function returns a local-time epoch ms via the Date(y, m-1, d) constructor, so results are timezone-dependent.
input.title: parseDate expects ISO YYYY-MM-DD, splits on '-'
input.evidence: Fixed bug where parseDate split on '/' returning NaN for '2026-06-14'. Verified: parseDate('2026-06-14') now returns 1781375400000.
